text: Audio engineering encompasses recording, mixing, and mastering sound. Key concepts: frequency (pitch, Hz), amplitude (loudness, dB), waveform (timbre). Recording chain: source → microphone → preamp → ADC → DAW. Mixing: balance levels, pan (stereo placement), EQ (adjust frequency balance), compression (control dynamics), reverb/delay (space).
Compression: ratio (e.g., 4:1 means 4dB input above threshold = 1dB output), attack, release.
Mastering: final polish — loudness normalization (LUFS for streaming: Spotify -14 LUFS).
